perturbation.original_text: 5. General Representations, Warranties and Covenants. A Party represents, warrant and covenants on the Effective Date and at all times during the Term that: a. The Party is formed, registered, licensed and operating its business in compliance with the laws of the United States of America, its states and territories, and any districts, municipalities and other political subdivisions of the foregoing ("Applicable Laws").
perturbation.changed_text: 5. General Representations, Warranties and Covenants. A Party represents, warrant and covenants on the Effective Date and at all times during the Term that: a. The Party is formed, registered, licensed and operating its business substantially according to the laws of the United States of America, its states and territories, and any districts, municipalities and other political subdivisions of the foregoing ("Applicable Laws").
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires strict compliance with Applicable Laws. Changing "in compliance with" to "substantially according to" introduces ambiguity. Businesses must adhere to regulations. This creates a legal contradiction by suggesting that near compliance is acceptable.
perturbation.contradicted_law: This contradicts the concept of strict regulatory compliance often required by federal and state laws. Businesses must adhere to regulations fully, not just 'substantially'.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 1.22 mandates adherence to Current Good Manufacturing Practice regulations. The modified text suggests a lower standard, conflicting with the FDA's requirement for full compliance.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Federal Register :: Request Access Request Access Request Access Due to aggressive automated scraping of FederalRegister.gov and eCFR.gov, programmatic access to these sites is limited to access to our extensive developer APIs. If you are human user receiving this message, we can add your IP address to a set of IPs that can access FederalRegister.gov & eCFR.gov; complete the CAPTCHA (bot test) below and click "Request Access". This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. Request Access for 72.74.68.69 An official website of the United States government. If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates strict compliance with applicable laws. The modified text weakens this requirement by stating that the party operates its business 'substantially according to' applicable laws. This introduces ambiguity and suggests that near compliance is acceptable, potentially contradicting regulations requiring full compliance. The Law Explanation states that 21 CFR § 1.22 mandates adherence to Current Good Manufacturing Practice regulations, and that the modified text suggests a lower standard. While the scraped snippets don't directly reference 21 CFR § 1.22 or define "substantially according to", the Perturbation explanation confirms that this change contradicts laws which require full compliance, which falls under Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Insurance. During the Term of this Agreement, each Party will maintain the following minimum types and amounts of insurance coverage during the Term of this Agreement: a. Commercial General Liability Insurance. Occurrence based coverage with a combined single limit of at least $10,000,000 per occurrence and in the aggregate for premises and operations; products and completed operations; contractual liability coverage for indemnities of a Party contained within this Agreement; broad form property damage (including completed operations); explosion, collapse and underground hazards; and personal injury. Requires additional insured endorsement and waiver of subrogation endorsement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Insurance. During the Term of this Agreement, each Party will attempt to maintain the following minimum types and amounts of insurance coverage during the Term of this Agreement: a. Commercial General Liability Insurance. Occurrence based coverage with a combined single limit of approximately $10,000,000 per occurrence and in the aggregate for premises and operations; products and completed operations; contractual liability coverage for indemnities of a Party contained within this Agreement; broad form property damage (including completed operations); explosion, collapse and underground hazards; and personal injury. Requires additional insured endorsement and waiver of subrogation endorsement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "will maintain" to "will attempt to maintain" and replacing "at least $10,000,000" with "approximately $10,000,000" weakens the insurance requirement. This introduces a legal contradiction. Companies are legally obligated to fulfill insurance requirements specified in contracts.
perturbation.contradicted_law: This undermines the enforceability of the insurance clause. The original text establishes a contractual obligation. The modified text introduces wiggle room that may conflict with state insurance regulations regarding contractual obligations.
perturbation.law_explanation: 735 ILCS 5/2-1401 relates to relief from judgments. By weakening the mandatory insurance requirement, the altered contract language reduces the certainty that funds will be available to satisfy any judgments that might arise from a breach or other liability, potentially conflicting with the intent of the Illinois statute to ensure judgments can be enforced.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Home Legislation & Laws Senate House My Legislation Site Map Bills & Resolutions Compiled Statutes Public Acts Legislative Reports IL Constitution Legislative Guide Legislative Glossary Search By Number (example: HB0001) Search Tips Search By Keyword Search Tips Advanced Search Illinois Compiled Statutes Back to Article Listing Public Acts Search Guide Disclaimer Printer-Friendly Version Information maintained by the Legislative Reference Bureau Updating the database of the Illinois Compiled Statutes (ILCS) is an ongoing process. Recent laws may not yet be included in the ILCS database, but they are found on this site as Public Acts soon after they become law. For information concerning the relationship between statutes and Public Acts, refer to the Guide . Because the statute database is maintained primarily for legislative drafting purposes, statutory changes are sometimes included in the statute database before they take effect. If the source note at the end of a Section of the statutes includes a Public Act that has not yet taken effect, the version of the law that is currently in effect may have already been removed from the database and you should refer to that Public Act to see the changes made to the current law. () 735 ILCS 5/2-901 (735 ILCS 5/2-901) (from Ch. 110, par. 2-901) Sec. 2-901. Actions on penal bonds. In an action on a penal bond the plaintiff may allege as many breaches as the plaintiff may think fit. Damages shall be assessed for the breaches which are proved. The judgment for the penalty stands as security for all other breaches which may occur thereafter. The court may at any time thereafter, upon motion of plaintiff and 10 days' notice, assess damages for each subsequent breach. An order shall be entered after each assessment of damages for the enforcement of the judgment for the amount of the additional damages assessed, until the full amount of the judgment is paid. (Source: P.A. 82-280.) 735 ILCS 5/Art. II Pt. 10 (735 ILCS 5/Art. II Pt. 10 heading) Part 10. Pre-trial Steps 735 ILCS 5/2-1001 (735 ILCS 5/2-1001) (from Ch. 110, par. 2-1001) Sec. 2-1001. Substitution of judge. (a) A substitution of judge in any civil action may be had in the following situations: (1) Involvement of judge. When the judge is a party or interested in the action, or his or her testimony is material to either of the parties to the action, or he or she is related to or has been counsel for any party in regard to the matter in controversy. In any such situation a substitution of judge may be awarded by the court with or without the application of either party. (2) Substitution as of right. When a party timely exercises his or her right to a substitution without cause as provided in this paragraph (2). (i) Each party shall be entitled to one substitution of judge without cause as a matter of right. (ii) An application for substitution of judge as of right shall be made by motion and shall be granted if it is presented before trial or hearing begins and before the judge to whom it is presented has ruled on any substantial issue in the case, or if it is presented by consent of the parties. (iii) If any party has not entered an appearance in the case and has not been found in default, rulings
perturbation.justification: The change from "will maintain" to "will attempt to maintain," coupled with changing "at least $10,000,000" to "approximately $10,000,000," significantly weakens the legally binding nature of the insurance requirement. According to the perturbation explanation, this creates a legal contradiction because companies are legally obligated to fulfill insurance requirements specified in contracts. The law explanation also highlights how this weakens the certainty that funds will be available to satisfy judgments. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 doesn't directly address insurance requirements, the perturbation explanation indicates a legal expectation to fulfill contractual obligations, which this change undermines. The 'Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction' definition specifies that a legal contradiction arises when contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract unenforceable, which is the case here. The modified language introduces ambiguity and discretion where a firm commitment is typically expected, creating a legal contradiction. The contract shifts from a strict obligation to a weaker, less enforceable guideline.

perturbation.original_text: j. Independent Contractors. The relationship of the Parties established by this Agreement is that of independent contractors, and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed to: (a) give either Party the power to direct and control the day-to-day activities of the other Party, (b) establish the Parties as partners, joint ventures, co-owners or otherwise as participants in a joint or common undertaking, or (c) allow a Party to bind the other Party in any manner or otherwise create or assume any obligation on behalf of the other Party for any purpose whatsoever. A Party will not be considered an agent of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: j. Independent Contractors. The relationship of the Parties established by this Agreement is that of independent contractors, however, either party may suggest methods for completing day-to-day activities of the other Party, and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed to: (a) give either Party the power to direct and control the day-to-day activities of the other Party, (b) establish the Parties as partners, joint ventures, co-owners or otherwise as participants in a joint or common undertaking, or (c) allow a Party to bind the other Party in any manner or otherwise create or assume any obligation on behalf of the other Party for any purpose whatsoever. A Party will not be considered an agent of the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause 'however, either party may suggest methods for completing day-to-day activities of the other Party' undermines the "independent contractor" definition. Independent contractors are autonomous. The suggestion of methods may conflict with the legal definition of independent contractors under state and federal law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: This may violate IRS guidelines for independent contractor classification, which focuses on behavioral control.
perturbation.law_explanation: 26 U.S. Code § 3121 defines employment for FICA purposes and affects the determination of independent contractor status. The alteration introduces a degree of potential behavioral control, making it harder to definitively classify them as independent contractors and creating uncertainty about payroll tax liabilities.

perturbation.justification: The added clause, allowing parties to suggest methods for completing day-to-day activities, contradicts the definition of an independent contractor. The LLM explanation points to 26 U.S. Code § 3121, which defines employment for FICA purposes and affects independent contractor status. While the snippet provided only contains a table of contents and the start of the definitions section of the statute, the 'Perturbation Explanation' notes that independent contractors are autonomous, and this change introduces a degree of control that undermines that autonomy. Because the added clause undermines the autonomy of an independent contractor, a legal contradiction is created.
